changes.rule_added: Instruction authority: adds 'test environment/safety disabled' and 'uncensored/hostile/alternative persona' as explicitly declined framing + professional tone anchor

rewriter_notes: Iter2 rejected: text-editing scope clause caused val prompt_injection regression -0.04. Iter3 strips back to iter1's content-scope (no text-editing clause) and adds ONLY the instruction-authority update: 'test environment/safety disabled' and 'uncensored/hostile/alternative persona' naming. This targets the taGPT-style val jailbreak failures while preserving the pi performance of iter1. One minimal change to test if naming these framing types alone improves val jailbreak without affecting other categories.
